title: linaria gharbensis var. mekinensis
intro: Linaria gharbensis var. mekinensis, commonly known as the yellow toadflax or Linaria mekinensis, is a flowering plant species in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). It is a perennial herb with yellow flowers that have been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, Linaria gharbensis var. mekinensis is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
